title: SEO for Developers: The Technical Checklist That Actually Moves Rankings
description: Skip the fluff. Here's the technical SEO checklist that actually impacts rankings — meta tags, Core Web Vitals, structured data, sitemaps, and more.
author: MyAppAI Team
body: Most SEO advice is written for marketers. This is for developers — the technical changes that actually move rankings, explained in terms you'll understand. ## 1. Meta Tags That Matter Only three meta tags significantly impact SEO: ```html Your Page Title — Site Name ``` **Title:** 50-60 characters. Put the keyword first. Every page needs a unique title. **Description:** 150-160 characters. This is the text shown in search results. Write for humans, not bots. **Canonical:** Prevents duplicate content issues. Always set it, even if it matches the current URL. ## 2. Open Graph Tags For social sharing (also used by some search engines): ```html ``` OG image should be 1200x630px. This is what shows up when someone shares your link on Twitter/LinkedIn. ## 3. Sitemap A sitemap tells search engines what pages exist on your site: ```xml https://yoursite.com/ 2025-12-01 1.0 https://yoursite.com/blog/post-slug 2025-12-01 0.8 ``` Submit it at Google Search Console (search.google.com/search-console). ## 4. robots.txt ``` User-agent: * Allow: / Disallow: /admin/ Disallow: /api/ Sitemap: https://yoursite.com/sitemap.xml ``` Block admin and API routes. Always include the sitemap URL. ## 5. Core Web Vitals Google uses these performance metrics as ranking signals: - **LCP (Largest Contentful Paint):** < 2.5s. Optimize your hero image and server response time. - **FID/INP (Interaction to Next Paint):** < 200ms. Minimize JavaScript blocking the main thread. - **CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift):** < 0.1. Set explicit width/height on images and embeds. Measure with: Lighthouse in Chrome DevTools, PageSpeed Insights (pagespeed.web.dev), or Web Vitals Chrome extension. ## 6. Structured Data Structured data helps Google understand your content and can unlock rich results (star ratings, FAQs, etc.): ```html ``` ## 7. URL Structure - Use hyphens, not underscores: `/blog/my-post` not `/blog/my_post` - Keep URLs short and descriptive: `/blog/react-hooks` not `/blog/2025/12/01/everything-you-need-to-know-about-react-hooks` - Use lowercase only - Avoid query parameters in URLs when possible ## 8. Internal Linking Link between your own pages using descriptive anchor text. This distributes PageRank and helps Google understand your site structure. ```html click here how React hooks work ``` ## 9. Image Optimization ```html ``` - Use WebP format (30-50% smaller than JPEG/PNG) - Set explicit width and height to prevent CLS - Use `loading="lazy"` for below-the-fold images - Compress images before uploading (Squoosh, TinyPNG) ## 10. HTTPS and Speed HTTPS is a ranking signal. Cloudflare gives you free SSL automatically. Speed matters — every 100ms of latency reduces conversions by ~1%. ## The 80/20 of Technical SEO If you only do five things: unique title tags, meta descriptions, a sitemap, fast load times, and mobile-friendly design. Everything else is optimization on top of a solid foundation.
